title: Distribution atom cache — per-connection short index encoding
purpose: Atoms are the most frequent term type in distribution messages. Sending the full atom string on every message wastes bandwidth. The distribution atom cache assigns short indices (0-255) to frequently used atoms per connection, so subsequent messages send the 1-byte index instead of the full string.
task: Implement the per-connection atom cache with cache entries, encoding, and decoding.

requirements.title: AtomCache per connection
requirements.spec: Each DistConnection maintains a bidirectional atom cache — one for encoding (local → remote) and one for decoding (remote → local).
acceptance: AtomCache struct with 256 slots (matching BEAM's cache size)
insert(atom: Atom) -> u8 assigns an index, returns it
lookup_by_index(index: u8) -> Option<Atom> retrieves cached atom
lookup_by_atom(atom: Atom) -> Option<u8> checks if atom is cached
Test: insert 256 atoms, verify all cached, insert 257th evicts first

requirements.title: Atom cache header encoding/decoding
requirements.spec: Implement the atom cache header format used in distribution messages to communicate new cache entries.
acceptance: Encode: when sending a message with an uncached atom, include a NEW_ATOM_CACHE_REF entry in the header
Decode: when receiving a message with cache header entries, update the local cache
Cached atoms are sent as 1-byte index references (ATOM_CACHE_REF)
Test: encode message with new atom, decode on other side, verify cache populated

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement atom cache for non-distribution contexts — this is connection-specific
SHALL NOT implement cache synchronization beyond the message header protocol
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
